original_hypothesis.title: Empty Watcher Verification Allows Unrestricted Withdrawals
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function. BaseConnector relies on this to validate withdrawal amounts requested by the accounting manager. A compromised accounting manager can extract the entire balance of any connector without Watcher intervention.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Security guardrails that are implemented as empty functions provide a false sense of security and fail to prevent catastrophic fund loss during a compromise.
deployment_assumptions: Accounting manager is compromised or has a vulnerability allowing arbitrary withdrawal requests
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment evidence required; inherent to contract logic.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function verifyRemoveLiquidity(...) external view { } has an empty body

preconditions: Accounting manager is compromised or manipulated

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No validation of sentAmount vs withdrawAmount
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Compromised accounting manager requests withdrawal of 100% of connector funds; Watcher silently approves

economic_questions: What is the intended logic for the Watcher verification?
validation_requirements: Verify that sendTokensToTrustedAddress succeeds regardless of the newAmount provided in data

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
